title: Funastrum dumetorum

intro: Funastrum dumetorum, also known as Ceballos' milkvine or Mexican climbing milkvine, is a species of flowering plant in the milkweed family (Asclepiadaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Funastrum dumetorum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Funastrum dumetorum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
